filename: Djeumou et al. (2022) - Neural Networks with Physics-Informed Architectures.md
title: Neural Networks with Physics-Informed Architectures and Constraints for Dynamical Systems Modeling
summary_abstract: The paper proposes a framework for learning dynamical system models from trajectory data by embedding available prior physics knowledge both into the neural network structure (as a compositional vector field) and as hard constraints enforced via the augmented Lagrangian method. Unknown terms in the equations of motion are parametrized by neural networks, which are composed with analytically known terms such as the mass matrix or Jacobian, then integrated via a differentiable ODE solver to produce multi-step rollout predictions. Experiments on a double pendulum and a suite of high-dimensional Brax robots show accuracy improvements of up to two orders of magnitude over a purely data-driven baseline.
key_results: (1) Compositional grey-box structure outperforms black-box baseline by 100x on rollout error with the same data. (2) Augmented Lagrangian constraint enforcement drives constraint violation to near zero (4 orders of magnitude lower than unconstrained). (3) Framework scales to 143-dimensional state spaces (Fetch robot). (4) Semi-supervised constraint enforcement on unlabeled state-space points improves out-of-distribution generalization.
keywords: physics-informed neural networks,augmented Lagrangian,grey-box dynamics,compositional vector field,neural ODE,rigid-body dynamics,contact force constraints,data efficiency,robotics
potential_novelties: N1: Compositional grey-box structure (known terms + neural residuals) directly maps to the project RNEA+tau_res architecture and validates the design choice. N2: Augmented Lagrangian training algorithm (Algorithm 1 with penalty escalation + Lagrange multiplier updates) is directly implementable for torque-limit and dissipativity constraints in Stage 1. N3: Semi-supervised constraint enforcement on unlabeled points throughout state-space could extend dissipativity constraints beyond training trajectories in Stage 1.
linked_goal: Goal 3 (scaling to 7-DoF); Goal 1 (URDF-to-model pipeline validation); Goal 4 (sim-to-real via physics constraints)
notes: Experiments use ReLU activations (project requires Mish/Softplus); dynamics are learned as full ODEs with differentiable solver (project uses offline qddot for RNEA, no ODE integration at training time); validated only in simulation (Brax), no real robot; no payload conditioning.

filename: Liu et al. (2024) - Physics-Informed Neural Networks to Model and Control Robots.md
title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks to Model and Control Robots: A Theoretical and Experimental Investigation
summary_abstract: Liu et al. extend Lagrangian and Hamiltonian Neural Networks (LNNs/HNNs) to handle non-conservative (dissipative) forces and non-collocated actuator inputs — two issues that blocked prior LNN/HNN work from applying to real robots. They replace the direct-acceleration loss with a Runge-Kutta-4 rollout loss to avoid needing unmeasurable joint accelerations, and prove Lyapunov stability for both a regulation and a trajectory-tracking controller derived from the learned model. Experiments are conducted on two soft continuum robots and on the Franka Emika Panda 7-DoF manipulator (simulation in PyBullet + real hardware tracking).
key_results: (1) LNN/HNN with damping matrix (D-NN) and actuator matrix (A-NN) sub-networks beats a black-box fully-connected baseline in trajectory prediction on all tested systems. (2) On the real Franka Panda at 500 Hz, prediction error drops from 182.5 to 2.68 rad^2 over 2 s vs. black box. (3) PD-augmented computed-torque controller tracks sinusoidal and helical trajectories on real Panda hardware. (4) Training uses only 25 000 samples for the Panda versus 550 000 for the black-box baseline. (5) No payload conditioning, no URDF-based white-box term, no torque-limit constraints, and no 1 kHz closed-loop demonstration are reported.
keywords: Lagrangian neural networks,Hamiltonian neural networks,damping matrix,non-collocated actuation,Runge-Kutta rollout loss,model-based control,Lyapunov stability,Franka Panda,soft robotics,physics-informed neural networks
potential_novelties: N1: RK4 rollout loss (predicting next state instead of current acceleration) avoids needing qddot at training time — applicable to the project Stage 1 training loop as an alternative or complement to the RNEA offline qddot path. N2: Cholesky-decomposition enforcement of positive-definiteness on the mass and damping sub-networks is a proven architecture trick directly adoptable for the tau_res dissipativity constraint in Stage 1. N3: Regulation+tracking controller proofs under learned-model uncertainty (bounded delta_all perturbation argument) provide a stability template for the Stage 3 computed-torque+PD controller. N4: Data efficiency benchmark (25k vs 550k samples for Panda) establishes a concrete comparison point for Stage 1 data requirements with URDF-seeded grey-box approach.
linked_goal: N1: Goal 4 (sim-to-real; removing non-causal qddot need); N2: Goal 3 (scaling to 7-DoF; physics constraint enforcement); N3: Goal 2 (real-time control stability guarantees); N4: Goal 1 (URDF pipeline data efficiency argument)
code_available: no — data available on request from authors
notes: PRIMARY BASELINE PAPER. Critical gaps vs. project goals: (1) No URDF-to-model automation — model equations are manually derived for each robot. (2) Control loop runs at 500 Hz on real robot; no 1 kHz MPC integration demonstrated. (3) Validated on Franka Panda (7-DoF) but without payload conditioning — cannot adapt to changing end-effector loads. (4) No systematic sim-to-real transfer protocol — hardware data collected directly without simulation pre-training. (5) Black-box LNN learns full dynamics from scratch; no analytical white-box RNEA term used. (6) No torque-limit or velocity-limit constraints in the loss. (7) Implemented in JAX/dm-Haiku, not PyTorch.
